step_1_context.text: After some verbs we need to include an object before a to-infinitive in active sentences:

step_2_explanation.simple_rule:
After some verbs we need to include an object before a to-infinitive in active sentences:
 • The police warned everyone to stay inside with their windows closed.
step_2_explanation.formula:
After some verbs we have to put a preposition, usually for, between the verb and the object + to-infinitive:
 • They arranged for Rania to stay in London.
step_2_explanation.common_mistake:
A number of other to-infinitive and -ing forms can also follow verbs:
 • verb + negative to-infinitive/negative -ing: We decided not to go to Paris after all.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 31.1.1 My mother ___ me to throw away my old toys.
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Threatened doesn't take an object before the infinitive.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 31.1.2 My mother ___ to throw away my old toys.
31.2.1 When I advertised ___ a website designer...
31.2.2 But I've now learnt that you can't rely ___ Greta to do anything.
31.3.1 I ___ anyone Marta's new address. (agree - not tell)
